title: Halosarcia lylei
taxonomy: Halosarcia lylei is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Amaranthaceae. It is native to Western Australia and is a succulent shrub. The plant belongs to the genus Halosarcia, which is part of the broader family Amaranthaceae. Halosarcia lylei is often found in sandy soils and is well adapted to arid environments. As a member of the Amaranthaceae family, it shares characteristics with other succulent plants found in Western Australia.

intro: Halosarcia lylei, also known as Lyle's glasswort, is a shrubby succulent plant species native to Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Halosarcia lylei include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
